nodes.context: Collection of organized crime groups composed of largely ethnic Irish members operating primarily in the United States since the early 19th century.
Irish-American street gang that dominated New York's underworld in the 19th century.
Irish-American street gang that dominated New York's underworld in the 19th century.
New York gang led by Paul Kelly that rose to prominence in the early 1900s.
New York gang that rivaled the Five Points Gang in the early 1900s.
New York gang that rivaled the Five Points Gang in the early 1900s.
Irish gang formed in early 1900s to counter Italian criminal organizations on the waterfront.
Italian criminal organization that encroached on the waterfront in early 1900s.
Irish American gang from Hell's Kitchen, Manhattan.
Italian Mafia family that sought control over the Jacob K. Javits Convention Center in the 1970s.
Italian Mafia family that allied with the Westies via Roy DeMeo.
Boston Irish gang led by Frank Wallace that dominated during Prohibition.
Loose confederation of Boston-area organized crime figures, active from early 1960s to mid-1990s.
Boston Irish gang led by the McLaughlin brothers, involved in the Irish Mob War of the 1960s.
South Boston Irish gang controlling bookmaking and loansharking in early 1970s.
South Boston Irish gang made up of thieves, rival to the Killeen Gang in early 1970s.
Prominent Irish street gang in Philadelphia pre-twentieth-century, headed by Jimmy Haggerty.
Dominant Irish gang in Philadelphia after WWII, based around Kensington and Allegheny.
Italian Mafia family in Philadelphia; soldier Raymond Martorano was indicted with K&A members in 1987.
Chicago Irish gang during Prohibition, including Dion O'Banion and Bugs Moran.
Italian mob organization led by Al Capone that rivaled Irish gangs during Prohibition.
Chicago Irish gang during Prohibition.
Chicago Irish gang during Prohibition.
Chicago Irish gang led by Myles O'Donnell during Prohibition.
Chicago Irish gang during Prohibition.
Chicago Irish gang during Prohibition.
Cleveland pre-prohibition gang from Irishtown Bend.
Cleveland pre-prohibition gang from Irishtown Bend.
Cleveland gang notorious for a robbery leading to murder of Detective William Hulligan.
Cleveland crime syndicate composed of Irish and Jewish gangsters, operated casinos in multiple states.
Italian mob in Cleveland, headed by Alfred Polizzi, associated with the Cleveland Syndicate.
Rival of the Irish Mob historically and depending on certain factions.
Prison gang listed as rival of the Irish Mob in Oklahoma.
Cleveland-based Italian-American organized crime group involved in garbage rackets and vending machines.
Danny Greene's crew operating in Cleveland.
Outlaw motorcycle gang; a member planted a car bomb for a Greene associate.
Mafia family; boss Jimmy Fratianno became a government informant.
Detroit-based gang; many kidnappings attributed to them were committed by the Laman gang.
Tommy Banks' bootlegging organization in Minneapolis.
Jewish-American organized crime mob led by Kid Cann and his brothers.
New Orleans prohibition gang from the Irish Channel neighborhood.
Mafia family; acting boss Silvestro Carollo ordered the murder of William Bailey.
Prison gang; Irish Mob in Oklahoma established as an alternative to them.
Prison gang that expanded into drug trafficking in Oklahoma, Kansas, and California.
John Patrick Looney's organization controlling gambling, prostitution, and extortion in Rock Island.
Rival gang to the Looney gang in Rock Island.
Prohibition-era gang controlling bootlegging in Southern Illinois.
Rival gang to the Shelton Brothers Gang in Southern Illinois.
White supremacist organization; engaged in war with Shelton Brothers and Birger gangs.
Large organized gang in St. Louis led by Thomas Egan and Thomas Kinney.
Rival gang to Egan's Rats in St. Louis led by Edward 'Jelly Roll' Hogan.
Toledo-based gang led by Thomas Licavoli; involved in turf war with Jack Kennedy.
Montreal-based Irish-Canadian organized crime group active since early 1900s.
Italian organized crime group in Montreal; ties to West End Gang.
Italian-American Mafia; ties to West End Gang.
Outlaw motorcycle gang; ties to West End Gang.
Colombian drug trafficking organizations; ties to West End Gang.

Irish American gang based in Hell's Kitchen, New York City.

edges.evidence_quote: The Five Points Gang (led by Paul Kelly) would rise to prominence during the early 1900s, strongly rivaled by the Hudson Dusters, the Gopher Gang, and others during the period.
edges.context: The Five Points Gang was strongly rivaled by the Hudson Dusters.

edges.evidence_quote: The Five Points Gang (led by Paul Kelly) would rise to prominence during the early 1900s, strongly rivaled by the Hudson Dusters, the Gopher Gang, and others during the period.
edges.context: The Five Points Gang was strongly rivaled by the Gopher Gang.

edges.evidence_quote: In the early 1900s, with Italian criminal organizations such as the Morello crime family encroaching on the waterfront, various Irish gangs united to form the White Hand Gang.
edges.context: The White Hand Gang formed to counter the Morello crime family's encroachment on the waterfront.

edges.evidence_quote: In the Irish/Italian Mob War of the 1970s, the Irish mob saw an increased threat from the Italian Mafia as the Genovese crime family sought control over the soon-to-be-built Jacob K. Javits Convention Center.
edges.context: The Genovese crime family sought control over the convention center, threatening the Westies.

edges.evidence_quote: This prompted Coonan to form an alliance with Roy DeMeo of the Gambino crime family.
edges.context: The Westies formed an alliance with the Gambino crime family via Roy DeMeo.

edges.evidence_quote: The Irish Mob War is the name given to conflicts throughout the 1960s between the two dominant Irish-American organized crime gangs in Massachusetts: the Charlestown Mob in Boston... and the Winter Hill Gang of Somerville...
edges.context: The Winter Hill Gang and the Charlestown Mob were in conflict during the Irish Mob War of the 1960s.

edges.evidence_quote: In the early 1970s, another mob war was taking place in South Boston between two other Irish-American gangs: the Killeen Gang... and the Mullen Gang...
edges.context: The Winter Hill Gang absorbed the remnants of the Killeen Gang after Donald Killeen was murdered.

edges.evidence_quote: In the early 1970s, another mob war was taking place in South Boston between two other Irish-American gangs: the Killeen Gang... and the Mullen Gang...
edges.context: The Winter Hill Gang absorbed the remnants of the Mullen Gang after the war.

edges.evidence_quote: In the early 1970s, another mob war was taking place in South Boston between two other Irish-American gangs: the Killeen Gang... and the Mullen Gang...
edges.context: The Killeen Gang and the Mullen Gang were in conflict in the early 1970s.

edges.evidence_quote: In 1987, Scarfo crime family soldier Raymond Martorano, Berkery, and dozens of others, were indicted for their involvement in a large methamphetamine ring.
edges.context: K&A Gang member John Berkery was indicted alongside Scarfo crime family soldier Raymond Martorano for a methamphetamine ring.

edges.evidence_quote: However, they would soon be rivaled by Italian mobsters, particularly Al Capone and the Chicago Outfit.
edges.context: The North Side Gang was rivaled by the Chicago Outfit during Prohibition.

edges.evidence_quote: Charles Polizzi the adopted brother of Alfred Polizzi, head of the Italian Mayfield Road Mob.
edges.context: Charles Polizzi, a member of the Cleveland Syndicate, was the adopted brother of Alfred Polizzi, head of the Mayfield Road Mob, indicating a cooperative relationship.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Italian-American Mafia (historically and depending on certain factions)
edges.context: The Irish Mob and the Italian-American Mafia are rivals historically and depending on certain factions.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: ... Aryan Brotherhood (in Oklahoma)
edges.context: The Irish Mob and the Aryan Brotherhood are rivals in Oklahoma.

edges.evidence_quote: Also, Mayfield Road Mob underboss, Frank 'Little Frank' Brancato employed Greene as muscle for the city's garbage rackets.
edges.context: Mayfield Road Mob employed Danny Greene, leader of The Celtic Club, as muscle.

edges.evidence_quote: Open warfare broke out between the Licavoli and Nardi factions. Greene backed John Nardi 's claim.
edges.context: The Celtic Club (Greene) backed John Nardi's faction against the Licavoli faction of Mayfield Road Mob.

edges.evidence_quote: Birns was murdered by a car bomb, which was planted by a Hell's Angel member who was hired by a Greene associate.
edges.context: A Hell's Angel member planted a car bomb that killed Alex Birns, an associate of Mayfield Road Mob.

edges.evidence_quote: Banks and Cann divided Minneapolis into territories with a handshake and worked side by side both during and after prohibition.
edges.context: Tommy Banks (Minneapolis Syndicate) and Kid Cann (Minneapolis Combination) divided territories and cooperated.

edges.evidence_quote: The Shelton Brothers Gang engaged in a war with Charles Birger 's gang that concluded, in 1925, when the Shelton Brothers were convicted of the murder of a mail carrier.
edges.context: The Shelton Brothers Gang and Charles Birger's gang were at war.

edges.evidence_quote: The Shelton Brothers Gang and rival Charles Birger gang engaged in a war with the Ku Klux Klan that concluded with a shoot out at Herrin.
edges.context: The Shelton Brothers Gang and Charles Birger gang fought against the Ku Klux Klan.

edges.evidence_quote: The Shelton Brothers Gang and rival Charles Birger gang engaged in a war with the Ku Klux Klan that concluded with a shoot out at Herrin.
edges.context: The Charles Birger gang and Shelton Brothers Gang fought against the Ku Klux Klan.

edges.evidence_quote: The gangs engaged in the Egan-Hogan War of 1921–23, which resulted in the breakup of Egan's Rats.
edges.context: Egan's Rats and Hogan Gang fought in the Egan-Hogan War.

edges.evidence_quote: The Looney gang entered into a war with William Gabel's gang.
edges.context: The Looney gang and William Gabel's gang were at war.

edges.evidence_quote: Since that time, the gang has formulated ties to the Montreal Mafia , the Cosa Nostra , the Hells Angels , and Colombian cartels.
edges.context: West End Gang has ties to Montreal Mafia.

edges.context: West End Gang has ties to Cosa Nostra.

edges.context: West End Gang has ties to Hells Angels.

edges.context: West End Gang has ties to Colombian cartels.

edges.evidence_quote: An Irish prison gang established itself as an alternative to the Aryan Brotherhood.
edges.context: The Oklahoma Irish Mob was established as an alternative to the Aryan Brotherhood.

edges.evidence_quote: In 2018 3 members of the Irish Mob would be arrested for intimidation witnesses in the deadly gang shootout between Aryan Brotherhood members and Irish Mob members near a motel in Oklahoma.
edges.context: Oklahoma Irish Mob and Aryan Brotherhood were involved in a deadly shootout.

edges.evidence_quote: Miller's Crossing (1990), Irish gangster Tom Reagan ( Gabriel Byrne ) tries to prevent a gang war between Irish boss Leo O'Bannion ( Albert Finney ) and Italian boss Johnny Casper ( Jon Polito ).
edges.context: The Irish Mob and Italian-American Mafia are depicted in a gang war.

edges.evidence_quote: Killing Them Softly (2012), based on the George V. Higgins book Cogan's Trade , about Irish American gangsters plotting to rob a high-stakes card game run by the Boston Italian-American Mafia .
edges.context: Irish American gangsters plot to rob a card game run by the Boston Italian-American Mafia.

edges.evidence_quote: Rage (2014), Irish crime families go to war with the Russian Bratva .
edges.context: Irish crime families are at war with the Russian Bratva.

edges.evidence_quote: Kill the Irishman (2011), in Cleveland in the 1970s, Danny Greene , an Irish American mob boss and FBI informant, goes to war against Cleveland crime family boss James T. Licavoli .
edges.context: Irish American mob boss Danny Greene goes to war against the Cleveland crime family.

edges.evidence_quote: In the seventh season of Sons of Anarchy (2013), IRA arms dealer Connor Malone goes rogue and eventually cuts ties with the organization permanently and establishes an independent gunrunning syndicate with other former IRA operatives.
edges.context: An IRA arms dealer cuts ties with the IRA and establishes an independent syndicate, but no direct relationship with the Irish Mob is stated.

edges.evidence_quote: This incarnation is led by a man named Tam Brodie and is at war with the local Triads .
edges.context: The Brodie Gang (Irish Mob) is at war with the local Triads.
